2606.10861.title: From Perception to Action: Can UI Interventions Foster Sustainable LLM Chatbot
2606.10861.paper_summary: LLM-powered chatbots are increasingly embedded in everyday workflows, raising sustainability concerns due to their energy use. Most mitigation strategies emphasize model or infrastructure efficiency, while the user-interface (UI) layer remains underexplored despite its potential to shape interaction behavior. We investigate whether sustainability-oriented UI interventions can increase users' energy awareness and encourage more energy-responsible chatbot use without reducing usability. We first conducted a baseline survey with 77 participants to assess awareness and receptiveness to intervention concepts. Guided by prior work on persuasive technology and choice architecture, we implemented a web-based chatbot prototype with a three-mode switch (Energy-efficient, Balanced, Performance), per-response energy feedback, pre-send energy estimates, a usage metrics dashboard, and energy analogies. We then evaluated the prototype in a five-day field study with 11 participants. In the baseline survey, 94.8% of respondents reported at least some awareness of AI energy use, yet 88.3% misestimated actual consumption. Although concern about environmental impact was high, only 39.0% indicated willingness to accept a performance trade-off for lower energy use. In the field study, Energy-efficient mode accounted for 55.8% of logged prompts, while 90.9% self-reported actively choosing Eco-mode when high accuracy was not required. Participants did not reduce prompt length, suggesting mode switching as the primary behavioral mechanism. Sustainability-oriented UI interventions can improve awareness and support more energy-responsible interaction patterns in LLM chatbots. These effects are best interpreted as behavioral and model-based estimates that complement backend efficiency work, and the provided prototype and replication package support further research on energy-aware conversational AI design.
2606.10861.paper_summary_zh: LLM 驅動的聊天機器人越來越多地嵌入日常工作流程中，這引發了對其能源使用的可持續性擔憂。大多數減緩策略強調模型或基礎設施的效率，而用戶界面（UI）層則仍然未被充分探索，儘管它具有塑造互動行為的潛力。我們研究了以可持續性為導向的 UI 介入是否能提高用戶的能源意識，並鼓勵更具能源責任感的聊天機器人使用，而不降低可用性。我們首先進行了一項基線調查，77 名參與者用於評估對介入概念的意識和接受度。根據之前在說服技術和選擇架構方面的研究，我們實施了一個基於網頁的聊天機器人原型，具有三種模式切換（節能、高效、性能）、每次響應的能源反饋、發送前的能源估算、使用指標儀表板和能源類比。然後，我們在為期五天的現場研究中評估了該原型，共有 11 名參與者。在基線調查中，94.8% 的受訪者報告至少對 AI 能源使用有一定的認識，但 88.3% 的人錯估了實際消耗。儘管對環境影響的擔憂很高，但只有 39.0% 的人表示願意接受性能的權衡以降低能源使用。在現場研究中，節能模式佔登記提示的 55.8%，而 90.9% 的人自報在不需要高準確率時主動選擇生態模式。參與者並未減少提示長度，這表明模式切換是主要的行為機制。以可持續性為導向的 UI 介入可以提高意識，並支持 LLM 聊天機器人中更具能源責任感的互動模式。這些效果最好被解釋為行為和基於模型的估算，補充後端效率工作，所提供的原型和複製包支持進一步研究能源意識的對話 AI 設計。
2606.10861.author: Nitish Patkar et.al.
2606.10861.authors: Nitish Patkar, Pooja Rani, Jack Glässer, Simon Lüscher, Martin Kropp
